location: Gaza Strip
notes: OCHA reported 23 Palestinians killed, 1 body retrieved, 1 person died of wounds, and 112 injured between 24 and 30 June, citing MoH figures.

location: Al Mawasi area of Khan Younis
notes: OCHA reported that a mother and her baby daughter were killed in an airstrike in Al Mawasi; the strike reportedly destroyed tents for more than 150 families and damaged those of an additional 250 families.

location: near a temporary education learning space
notes: OCHA reported that a fifth-grade child died from injuries sustained during an airstrike on 23 June near a temporary education learning space.

location: West Bank
notes: OCHA reported that Israeli forces killed three Palestinians (including one child) during raids and search operations in the West Bank between 23 and 29 June; about 20 Palestinians were injured.

location: eastern Deir al Balah governorate and eastern Gaza city
notes: OCHA reported that more than 20 families were displaced from eastern Deir al Balah governorate and more than a dozen from eastern Gaza city between 26 and 27 June after people were ordered to leave or fled as forces advanced.

location: Wadi Salman Bedouin community in Area C of Ramallah governorate
notes: OCHA reported that a Palestinian Bedouin household of eight people, including four children and three women, was displaced from Wadi Salman following repeated settler attacks and relocated to a safer area.

location: Shufat Camp
notes: OCHA reported that Israeli authorities demolished 12 livelihood structures in Shufat Camp, affecting 12 households comprising 48 people and damaging commercial shops.

location: Gaza Strip - 130 health facilities across territory
notes: OCHA reported that in just two weeks, over 9,000 cases of chickenpox were recorded across more than 130 health facilities in Gaza, with over half in Khan Younis, driven by deteriorating environmental conditions and overcrowding.

location: Al Shifa Hospital - Gaza City
notes: OCHA reported a critical disruption in haemodialysis services at Al Shifa Hospital due to shortage of sodium bicarbonate, reducing treatment capacity for approximately 240 patients with end-stage kidney disease and requiring reduction of dialysis sessions.

location: West Bank - multiple communities
notes: OCHA reported that between January 2023 and 29 June 2026, more than 6,200 Palestinians (including over 3,000 children) have been displaced due to settler attacks and access restrictions, with over 2,300 people in 2026 alone.

location: Al Bowereh and Hiwara communities in Hebron governorate
notes: OCHA documented that establishment of settlement outposts near Al Bowereh (120+ families) and Hiwara (30 households) was accompanied by repeated settler attacks on homes, agricultural land, and livelihood assets, with at least 6 documented attacks in Al Bowereh and 11 in Hiwara.

location: Kafr Aqab
notes: OCHA reported that Israeli authorities demolished a multi-storey Palestinian-owned building in Kafr Aqab on 22 June, displacing two households and destroying property on the West Bank side of the Barrier.

location: East Jerusalem areas West Bank side of Barrier
notes: OCHA documented that in 2026, 37% of structures demolished in East Jerusalem for lacking Israeli-issued building permits were located on the West Bank side of the Barrier, nearly double the six-year average of 19%.

location: Kafr ad Dik in Salfit governorate
notes: OCHA documented that on 28 June, settlers established a new tent and threatened five Palestinian herding families, prompting them to relocate approximately 400 sheep and other belongings.

location: Rammun village in Ramallah governorate
notes: OCHA reported that Israeli settlers cut electricity cables connected to an under-construction school and broke into several agricultural structures in Rammun, stealing farming equipment in an area where a nearby settlement outpost has restricted Palestinian farmers access to agricultural land.

location: Jalud village in Nablus governorate
notes: OCHA documented that on 29 June, Israeli settlers occupied an unfinished Palestinian house in Jalud village.

location: Khan al Ahmar in Jerusalem governorate
notes: OCHA reported that residents in Khan al Ahmar reported near-daily settler incursions involving damage to water and electricity infrastructure, harassment, and grazing of livestock on community land.

location: East Jerusalem - Kafr Aqab and Shufat Camp
notes: OCHA reported that 14 Palestinian-owned structures were demolished during the reporting period for lacking Israeli-issued building permits, including 12 in East Jerusalem and 2 in Area C, affecting multiple households.

location: UNRWA Kalandia Training Centre (KTC) in East Jerusalem
notes: OCHA reported that on 30 June, Israeli forces forcibly entered the UNRWA Kalandia Training Centre, inspected premises, and informed staff that the vocational training facility providing free education to hundreds of students would be required to close; the UN condemned the breach of inviolability.

location: Kalandiya Camp
notes: A Palestinian child was shot and killed by Israeli forces during a search operation; two other children were injured in the same incident. (Merged from phase1_new_events; original draft event_id EVT-014 renumbered to avoid collision.)

location: Kerem Shalom to Gaza warehouse route
notes: A driver working for a World Central Kitchen logistics partner was killed by Israeli forces while transporting aid goods within an access-restricted area, in a movement reportedly coordinated with Israeli authorities. (Merged from phase1_new_events; original draft event_id EVT-015 renumbered.)

location: Gaza Strip (29 June-5 July reporting period)
notes: Per Gaza Ministry of Health, 44 Palestinians were reported killed during the 29 June-5 July reporting period, including those who died of previous wounds. (Merged from phase1_new_events; original draft event_id EVT-016 renumbered.)

location: West Bank including East Jerusalem (cumulative since 7 Oct 2023)
notes: Cumulative OCHA-recorded figure: 1,109 Palestinians (at least 243 of them children) killed in the West Bank including East Jerusalem between 7 October 2023 and 29 June 2026; 63 of these deaths occurred in 2026 alone. (Merged from phase1_new_events; original draft event_id EVT-017 renumbered.)

location: Gaza Strip (post-ceasefire cumulative)
notes: Cumulative figure covering the period from the ceasefire announcement on 10 October 2025 through 30 June 2026: 1,053 fatalities and 3,406 injuries recorded from continued Israeli airstrikes, shelling, and gunfire despite the ceasefire. (Merged from phase1_new_events; original draft event_id EVT-018 renumbered.)

location: West Bank, near Ramallah (Israeli-staffed road gate)
notes: Transfer of a four-month-old Palestinian infant to hospital was delayed at an Israeli-staffed road gate near Ramallah, where movement restrictions have isolated roughly 12,000 Palestinians; the infant later died at the hospital. (Merged from phase1_new_events; original draft event_id EVT-019 renumbered.)

location: Gaza Strip (water access)
notes: OCHA reported that insufficient funding is threatening the trucked bottled-water supply relied on by roughly 70 percent of Gazas population. (Merged from phase1_new_events; original draft event_id EVT-020 renumbered.)

location: Gaza Strip (shelter recovery)
notes: Aggregate shelter recovery figure: emergency repairs completed on 453 partially damaged homes in the reporting period, bringing the cumulative number of rehabilitated housing units to approximately 1,299 since the start of 2026. (Merged from phase1_new_events; original draft event_id EVT-021 renumbered.)
